research_basis: AWS Reliability Pillar requires defining RTO/RPO, recovery strategies, testing DR implementation, managing drift, and automating recovery.
AWS cutover runbook guidance recommends rollback planning when migration exceeds outage windows and recording observations/troubleshooting.
NIST contingency planning describes recovery strategies, contingency plans, testing/training/exercises, and maintenance.
rules: Recovery objectives must include RTO, RPO, rollback window, data loss tolerance, and success state.
At least one failure mode must require rollback.
Rollback plan must include trigger conditions, steps, owner, verification, and abort conditions.
Recovery runbook steps must include owner, expected result, and evidence.
At least one validation drill is required.
Drift controls and cleanup plan are mandatory.
